Gaelic Football · Attacking Theme · Full Pitch

Building a Score

Attacking Theme
Full Squad
Full Pitch
4 Phases
🎯 Session Principle
Do It or Done
Every possession has a decision point — give & go, finish your run, receive moving. If you don’t do it, you’ve cost the team. The scoring system in the main game rewards sustained attack, not individual brilliance.
Theme
Building a score — sustained attacking possession & finishing
Format
Murderball full game + SSG “Do it or Done” rotating across 3 groups
Key Rule
Score = you keep the ball. Wide = coach decides re-start. Build the attack.
Ball in Play
6 ball re-entry points on pitch. Game never fully stops.
WU
MURDER­BALL
DO IT
OR DONE
ROTATION BLOCKS ×3
Phase 01
Warm-Up
~15 min
Phase 02
Murderball
~20 min

Rotation Blocks

Phase 01 · Warm Up · Pitch
Warm-Up
~15 min
Movement Circuit — Coach Led
  • Movement CircuitCoach led
⏱ Movement circuit primes the body for the explosive work ahead. Full range on every rep — don’t rush it.
Skills WU — In 3’s — Infused with Plyos & Bounds
  • FormatGroups of 3 · with ball
  • Plyos: vertical hops, mini bounds, pogo’s, skater jumps — infused between ball work
  • Dummy solo — show & bounce
  • Outside of boot kick — add this to the cycle
  • Throw up to break — high ball delivery, contest the break
📌 The plyos and ball skills are interleaved — not done separately. Alternating physical and skill tasks keeps the nervous system sharp and mirrors the demands of the game.
3 B’s of Support Play — 6-Man Group Runs
  • FormatGroups of 6 · full pitch · unopposed
  • Behind — run in behind the last line, creating depth and a goal threat
  • Beside — support on the shoulder of the ball carrier, immediate option
  • Before — in front of the ball carrier, short option to keep possession moving forward
📌 This is an unopposed WU practice — no coaching intervention. Players explore the 3 B’s in context before the competitive phases begin. No plays allowed.
Plyos in the WU are low load — quality foot contact, not maximum height or distance
3 B’s runs: name what you see as it happens — Behind, Beside, Before. Language before the game.
Keep the energy high coming out of WU — Murderball starts immediately after
1 / 4
Phase 02 · Full Pitch · Build a Score
Murderball — Build a Score
~20 min
Game Rules
  • FormatFull game · full pitch
  • Ball re-entry points6 areas on pitch
  • Score = keep ballCollect from any of 6 points
  • Behind goalRe-starts as a kickout
  • Sideline ballStandard sideline rules
  • WideCoach places ball — opposing team re-starts
⚽ Ball Re-Entry Points — 6 Areas
🔥 If you score, you collect a ball from any of the 6 points and attack immediately. The game never fully stops — tempo of attack is part of the challenge. A wide means the opposition re-starts from a position called by the coach.
Murderball Setup
▣

JPG / PNGMP4URL
URL
Supported: JPG, PNG, MP4, WebM — or paste a direct URL
Score and immediately collect — the team that goes again quickest controls the game
Wides are punished by the coach placing the ball — make this count. Poor shot selection = ball goes to opposition in a favourable position.
Watch for teams who switch off after scoring — the game never stops
2 / 4
Phase 03 · SSG · In-Possession Principles
“Do It or Done”
~15 min
Game Format
  • Format7v7 (or numbers available)
  • Structure4 × 3 min games · 1 min rest + team chat
  • Turnover ruleSolo & go within 4 secs or turnover free
📌 The coach or ref monitors each of the 3 principles below. Any failure = turnover free against the offending team. No warnings — the rule is live from the first second of each game.
The 3 In-Possession Principles
01
Give & Go
Pass and immediately move. Don’t stand and watch the ball — your next position is as important as your pass.
02
Finish Your Runs
Once you’ve started a run, commit to it fully. Half-finished runs create neither space nor options — they just block the play.
03
Receive Moving
Catch or collect the ball while in motion. Receiving flat-footed removes your options and gives the defender time to react.
4-Second Solo Rule
  • RuleSolo & go within 4 seconds of receiving
  • PenaltyTurnover free if not complied with
⚡ The 4-second rule forces decisive movement from the moment of reception. Combined with “receive moving”, it removes hesitation entirely — the ball keeps moving or the team pays the price.
Call violations immediately and clearly — no retrospective calls. Instant consequences only.
1 min between games is for team chat — let the players identify which principle is breaking down before the coach says anything
4 × 3 min is short enough to play at full intensity every game — hold that standard
Don’t over-coach the principles mid-game. Let the turnover free do the teaching.
3 / 4
Phase 04 · Full Pitch + SSG Areas · 3 Groups
Rotation Blocks — Full Game + SSG
~45 min
Structure
  • Groups3 groups rotating
  • Full game~12 min · 2 groups on full pitch
  • SSG / Waves~12 min · 1 group off pitch
  • RotateAfter each block
🔁 3 groups, 3 blocks. Everyone gets 2 full games and 1 SSG/Waves block. Full game applies Murderball rules from Phase 02. SSG applies Do It or Done rules from Phase 03.

URL
Supported: JPG, PNG, MP4, WebM — or paste a direct URL
Full game applies Murderball rules from Phase 02 — score and go again immediately. No dead time.
SSG slot applies the 3 principles from Phase 03 — turnover free still enforced
Keep transitions between blocks fast — 90 seconds maximum changeover
Waves group: attack first then defend. Coaches can adjust constraints block-to-block based on what the full game is showing up.
Don’t score-watch — watch the principles. Are they giving and going? Finishing runs? Receiving moving?
